title: dtn.c: Buffer accounting uses MESSAGE_OVERHEAD constant that may drift from struct size
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/dtn.c
Lines: 15, 20-23

Buffer accounting uses a hardcoded overhead:

    #define MESSAGE_OVERHEAD 100

    static uint32_t message_size(const struct lichen_dtn_message *msg)
    {
        return (uint32_t)msg->packet_len + MESSAGE_OVERHEAD;
    }

But sizeof(struct lichen_dtn_message) is:
- packet[1280] + packet_len(2) + destination_iid[8] + expiry_unix(4) + buffered_at_ms(4) + valid(1) + padding
- Actual: ~1300+ bytes

The MESSAGE_OVERHEAD=100 doesn't account for the struct's actual size.

When max_bytes=65536 (64KB default) and actual message struct is ~1300 bytes:
- Code thinks it can fit 65536/(packet_len+100) messages
- But actual memory usage per slot is 1300+ bytes always

This causes the buffer to use more memory than max_bytes suggests, or fail to accept messages that should fit.

Fix: message_size should return sizeof(struct lichen_dtn_message) or use actual struct overhead.
close_reason: Fixed buffer accounting to use sizeof(struct lichen_dtn_message)=1304 instead of packet_len+100. Updated message_size() and precompute in lichen_dtn_buffer_message(). Removed obsolete MESSAGE_OVERHEAD. Matches struct layout and prevents undercounting memory use. Verified with sizeof check and commit b3d72c380.
